title: PM strategy-ready discovery packet
hypothesis: If ProductOS carries explicit mission, strategy, and product-vision context into downstream discovery, a PM can choose the next bet faster and with less reconstruction.
positioning_hypothesis: ProductOS should position itself as the PM operating layer that makes strategy, evidence, and downstream artifacts traceable enough to trust.
offering_hypothesis: The offering should behave as a strategy and workflow decision layer instead of a generic all-in-one PM suite.
wedge_hypothesis: A strategy-ready discovery packet is the sharpest entry wedge because it improves PM judgment before trying to automate everything else.
why_now: AI-assisted drafting is raising the cost of weak strategic framing because more output can now be generated from unclear upstream intent.
why_us: ProductOS already centers structured artifacts, validation, and workflow state, so it can preserve strategy context more credibly than prompt-only systems.
advantage_hypothesis: Combining explicit strategy contracts with downstream traceability can make PM-facing discovery packets trusted enough for real product decisions.
must_be_true_assumptions: PMs will trust a strategy packet more when mission, vision, and market posture are explicit and linked
The strategy spine can sharpen downstream framing without forcing a new UI or broader release claim
Strategy-ready packets can coexist with existing PM tools instead of requiring full workflow replacement first
open_questions: Which sections of the strategy-ready packet should be mandatory in the first PM-facing discover output?
